rationale: Neither candidate catches the substantive ground-truth concern (dsocek's suggestion to derive the naming/mapping from `_packed_modules_mapping`/`stacked_params_mapping`), though both independently converge on the adjacent 'derive default_components from `_dit_modules`' nit with solid grounding (pipeline_sdxl.py, registry.py, interface.py). X delivers a clean, well-calibrated review (COMMENT verdict matching the actual merged/approved outcome) plus a genuinely valid extra finding (silent no-op for text-encoder LoRA). Y's output is marred by verbatim triplication of the entire review_text and verdict, and lands on REQUEST CHANGES for what are objectively a minor/nit pair on an already twice-LGTM'd, merged PR — a miscalibrated severity call that hurts practical usefulness despite individually grounded claims.
